content:
The user wants me to extract the content of the image and format it as JSON.

**1. Analyze the image:**
The image is a technical document, likely a datasheet or specification sheet for a semiconductor component.
- **Title:** SOT-23 (TO-236) 2.90x1.30x1.00 1.90P CASE 318 ISSUE AU
- **Date:** DATE 14 AUG 2024
- **Content:** It lists various "STYLE" configurations and their corresponding pinouts (PIN 1, 2, 3).

- **Footer:**

    - DESCRIPTION: SOT-23 (TO-236) 2.90x1.30x1.00 1.90P
    - PAGE 2 OF 2
    - Legal text about trademarks and liability.
    - Copyright notice: © Semiconductor Components Industries, LLC, 2019
    - Website: www.onsemi.com

**2. Structure the JSON:**
I will create a list of dictionaries, where each dictionary represents a block of text. I'll include the text and its bounding box.

- Title block
- Date block
- The main table-like structure with styles and pinouts. I'll format this as text blocks for each style group.
- Footer blocks (Document Number, Description, Page, Legal text, Copyright, Website).

Let's refine the bounding boxes.
- Title: ~[360, 64, 635, 105]
- Date: ~[760, 104, 896, 118]
- Styles: There are multiple columns. I'll group them logically.
    - Column 1: STYLE 1, STYLE 9, STYLE 15, STYLE 21, STYLE 27
    - Column 2: STYLE 6, STYLE 10, STYLE 16, STYLE 22, STYLE 28
    - Column 3: STYLE 7, STYLE 11, STYLE 17, STYLE 23
    - Column 4: STYLE 8, STYLE 12, STYLE 18, STYLE 24
    - Column 5: STYLE 13, STYLE 19, STYLE 25
    - Column 6: STYLE 14, STYLE 20, STYLE 26
- Footer:
    - Document Number row
    - Description row
    - Page row
    - Legal text block
    - Copyright row
    - Website row

I will combine the text for each style to make it readable.
